Alright, JB's in the house for your daily motivation slash skills training and guess what today is? Throwback Thursday. Alright, let's rock on. I got a great clip for you here for customer's love. You're going to love this one. Alright, it's a story. Alright, so listen up, enjoy it and we'll talk again tomorrow. Take care. Use gifts correctly. The best example of this I've ever heard was a guy at Ford Motor Company. The guy from Ford tells the story how there was a buyer who was trying to sell Ford Motor Company rubber parts for years and years as he could to use a count for it. If you get into Ford it's like just a massive business, right? Then Ford had a supplier that he was happy with and he didn't have business with this other guy but the other guy would wind him and dine him for years trying to break the account and couldn't get it. One day while they're out for lunch and he's winding and dying this guy from Ford, he happens to get into the conversation of football and the guy from Ford tells the salesman, yeah my son loves this football player in Chicago, he's a famous quarterback, he idolizes them, well what just so happened that this guy had a relationship with someone who knew that quarterback. He got that quarter back to sign a football, okay a $40 football to dear Johnny from yours truly the guy to his son, remember the family name. He then shipped the football to the Ford Motor guy and said here I thought this would be really nice for your son, best wishes Joe. The guy from Ford was so blown away, he called them back and said wow I just can't, even my son got this football, he was crying, he was this idol of all time, listen I don't have anything for you right now but just thank you so much. Four weeks later he landed the rubber account for like $180 million account from a $40 football. That's how you use gifts correctly. At the heart of all great gift giving is this one single concept, it's called identifying your clients aliveness. What makes them feel alive outside of the office, for example the worst Christmas gift you can give a plumber is a wrench. Happy Christmas is a wrench. I say douchebag, I gotta remember. The best gift you can give a plumber is maybe tickets to a sporting event that he loves. Let's say you happen to know that he's a wild rugby fan and you give him tickets to this great game of his favorite team, he's gonna say wow that's unbelievable, thank you so much, it matters to him, it matters to his aliveness but let's say you didn't take the time out to find out what his favorite team is and you gave it to the team he hates. And it was Collingwood and he was another fan, everyone hates Collingwood right? You gave Collingwood that douchebag, he hates you, he'll hate you for giving the wrong gift. Give your gifts carefully, find out what they like specifically and fill that need. Don't give your gifts randomly, give your gifts carefully calculated, people have different interests, everyone has their own hobbies and stuff that makes them feel alive outside of work. An example of a great gift would be, let's say you have someone who loves going around the world and you know they love to go and travel and eat great food. A nice living room, you know, coffee table book of great restaurants of the world, something like that, or great travel locations of the world. Let's say you have a client who loves to go hunting and fishing, a one year subscription to Field and Stream magazine, every month it comes to his door courtesy of you. That is a magazine that matters to that person. But if you're not going to send Field and Stream to some Martha Stewart lover, right? You get it, you have to know what makes someone feel alive and nothing makes them feel more alive than when their children smile.
